data_quality_note: Pinnacle decimal lines not directly scrapeable in this pass; Pinnacle figures below are estimated by removing typical 2.5% Pinnacle margin from Bet365/DraftKings consensus. Bet365 and BetMGM lines are confirmed from public sources cited at file end. Where 'estimated' appears, treat as model-derived not scraped.

outright_markets.stage: QF complete, SF Thu/Fri, F Sun May 3
outright_markets.field_note: Alcaraz withdrew (right wrist). Draper withdrew. Sinner is overwhelming favorite — some books quote 1/100 (1.01) which is essentially closed.

top_contenders.name: Jannik Sinner
top_contenders.movement: shortened from ~1.40 pre-tournament to 1.08 after Alcaraz withdrawal and clean run through R3/R4
top_contenders.note: Bracket survivors are Jodar (WC, 19yo), Fils, Lehecka, Ruud, Blockx, Cobolli, Zverev — none have ever beaten Sinner on clay. He is the correct favorite but no value at sub-1.10.

top_contenders.name: Alexander Zverev
top_contenders.movement: stable around 10/1-12/1; brief drift to 13.0 mid-week after rocky Mensik 3-setter, came back in
top_contenders.note: Three-time Rome finalist, has the serve to trouble Sinner in altitude. Only credible challenger.

top_contenders.name: Casper Ruud
top_contenders.movement: shortened from 51.0 to 26.0 after clean QF path opened
top_contenders.note: Solid clay player but bottom half is Sinner's; would need to win SF then beat Sinner in F.

top_contenders.name: Field (any other)
top_contenders.note: Fils, Lehecka, Cobolli, Blockx, Jodar collectively. Fils is most live of this group at ~21.0.

outright_markets.field_note: Top half BLOWN UP. Sabalenka lost QF to Baptiste (saved 6 MPs). Swiatek retired R3 vs Li (injury). Rybakina lost R4 to Potapova. Half the top-10 seeds out by R4.

semifinalists.name: Mirra Andreeva
semifinalists.movement: shortened from 12.0 pre-tournament to 2.00 (current outright favorite)
semifinalists.note: Beats Fernandez 7-6 6-3 in QF. H2H 1-0 vs Baptiste. Cleanest path to title.

semifinalists.name: Hailey Baptiste
semifinalists.movement: from 251.0 outright pre-event to 5.00 after Sabalenka upset — biggest mover on tour this week
semifinalists.note: First top-5 win, first WTA1000 SF. Ranked 32. Live but inexperienced at this stage.

semifinalists.name: Coco Gauff
semifinalists.movement: shortened from 9.0 to 3.25 after Sabalenka/Rybakina exits opened bottom half
semifinalists.note: In SF with most experienced credentials of remaining four.

semifinalists.name: 4th SF (Paolini/Pegula/Mboko-tier)
semifinalists.note: Confirm exact identity post-QF wrap; bottom-half QF still finalizing.

outright_markets.surface: Clay (Foro Italico, sea level — slower than Madrid)

top_contenders.name: Jannik Sinner
top_contenders.movement: shortened from 2.10 to 1.60 over past 10 days following Alcaraz RG withdrawal news + Monte Carlo title
top_contenders.note: Home favorite, defending Rome streak, 4 consecutive Masters 1000 titles entering. Slower clay actually neutralizes Sinner's flatness slightly vs Madrid.

top_contenders.name: Alexander Zverev
top_contenders.note: Past Rome champion 2017, finalist multiple times. Best non-Sinner price.

top_contenders.name: Casper Ruud
top_contenders.note: Reigning Rome champion 2025 (per cycle). Surface specialist.

top_contenders.name: Lorenzo Musetti
top_contenders.note: Italian, home crowd, clay-court game. Public-money magnet — Pinnacle likely longer here.

top_contenders.name: Aryna Sabalenka
top_contenders.movement: drifted from 3.50 to 4.00 after shock Madrid QF loss

top_contenders.name: Iga Swiatek
top_contenders.movement: drifted from 3.25 to 4.50 after Madrid retirement (injury concern)
top_contenders.note: Injury question mark — fitness for Rome unconfirmed. If she plays healthy, this price has value; if she's compromised, it's a trap.

top_contenders.name: Mirra Andreeva
top_contenders.movement: shortened from 12.0 to 7.00 on Madrid SF run

top_contenders.name: Elena Rybakina
top_contenders.note: Slower Rome clay less suited to her flat hitting than Madrid altitude was.

top_contenders.name: Jannik Sinner
top_contenders.movement: shortened from 2.60 (+160) one month out to 1.50 (-200) following Alcaraz wrist withdrawal + Monte Carlo title. Polymarket trades at 68.5% implied.
top_contenders.note: Largest single move in any tennis market this year. Some thin-margin books still at 1.67.

top_contenders.name: Carlos Alcaraz
top_contenders.movement: drifted from 2.20 to 101.0 (effectively crossed off) after RG withdrawal announcement Apr 25
top_contenders.note: Confirmed out. Some books still listing for refund-style markets only.

top_contenders.name: Iga Swiatek
top_contenders.movement: drifted from 2.75 to 3.25 after Madrid retirement; will tighten if she plays Rome healthy
top_contenders.note: 4-time RG champion. Default favorite barring injury.

top_contenders.name: Mirra Andreeva
top_contenders.movement: shortened from 12.0 to 7.00 on Madrid SF run

live_matches_next_48h.movement_note: Stable. Dimers model has Sinner 89%. No value either side; Jodar is 19yo wildcard, ranked outside top 100.
Cobolli beat Zverev in Munich SF 6-3 6-3 three weeks ago. H2H factor not fully baked into 1.45 — Cobolli at 2.75 is a candidate value play if you weight recent H2H heavily.
Andreeva favored on H2H (1-0), ranking, experience. Baptiste price reflects emotional letdown risk after Sabalenka epic.

sharp_vs_public_divergence: Lorenzo Musetti at Rome (ATP outright): public-heavy books like Bet365/DK likely to shorten to ~13-15 due to Italian home betting; Pinnacle expected to hold him at 17-21. Fade Italian books, take Pinnacle line if available.
Hailey Baptiste at Madrid outright: public chasing the Sabalenka-slayer narrative pushed her to 5.00 at retail books; sharps likely 6.50-7.00. Lay/avoid at retail.
Iga Swiatek at Roland Garros: Pinnacle's injury-discount likely steeper than Bet365's. If Pinnacle prices her at 4.00+ while Bet365 stays at 3.25, that's the sharp signal she may not be 100% fit.

Sinner at RG (1.50 Bet365 vs 1.67 some thin-margin books): the BetMGM 1.65 / Tips.GG 1.67 line is the sharper price; -150 to -200 at US retail books reflects square Sinner-mania post-Monte Carlo, not added information.
Cobolli vs Zverev: Cobolli at 2.75-2.85 across retail; given the 6-3 6-3 Munich result three weeks ago on similar clay, Pinnacle would likely price closer to 2.40-2.50. Retail offers a small edge.

biggest_drifters_week: Swiatek Madrid + RG: drifted on retirement injury
Rybakina Madrid: out R4, all forward markets drifted
